what_it_does: Stores an array of subtle visual-design findings extracted from pitch-deck and landing-page materials. Each entry documents a pattern name, category, observed recipe, evidence, emergent flag, prescriptive generator rule, source artifact, and compression depth.
observations: Each finding pairs an observed pattern with a prescriptive generator_rule.
A boolean emergent flag distinguishes discovered patterns from explicit design choices.
Categories cluster around gradient/shading/opacity, chrome-mode, texture, and emergent directional/temporality semantics.
Evidence cites slide or page references rather than code or image coordinates.
_src and _depth fields tie every rule to a specific artifact and compression level.
Many entries concern background textures, photo overlays, device mockups, stat bands, and chrome-mode transitions.
Some rules assign semantic meaning to visual channels, such as opacity encoding future states or gradients encoding directional axes.
The file spans multiple source types including 1-page, 15-page, 30-page pitch decks and web landing pages.
There are source-specific contradictions, for example cover overlays versus no overlays, warm-cream versus pure-white grounds, and warm versus cool photo grades.
standouts: Opacity and gradient direction are used as semantic encodings for temporality and conceptual axes.
An architectural ghost watermark or blueprint hatch appears repeatedly as a substrate texture across many sources.
Chrome modes are treated as narrative register shifts, distinguishing immersive photo, content, catalogue, data-dense, and gallery modes.
Screenshot collage treatment is used as a rhetorical device, where chaotic layering signals the problem and ordered framing signals the solution.
The deck body is light mode while product UI screenshots use dark chrome to signal software inside the document.
conventions: Each entry follows a fixed schema: category, name, recipe, evidence, emergent, generator_rule, _src, _depth.
Every finding includes an actionable generator_rule.
Evidence cites specific slide or page identifiers.
Source and depth metadata are attached to every finding.
A warm gold, amber, and cream palette is used consistently.
Deck slides and web landing pages are distinguished by source metadata.
The emergent flag marks discovered versus specified patterns.
concerns: Rules from different sources contradict each other, such as overlay/no-overlay covers and warm-cream versus pure-white backgrounds.
The placeholder-block-flat-gray entry explicitly notes it is a WIP convention not meant for final output.
Hex and opacity values are approximate and vary across sources.
There is no versioning or confidence score for conflicting rules.
Evidence is slide/page references without image hashes or bounding boxes, so reproducibility depends on external context.
Some generator rules are tightly coupled to a single source or depth.
notes: This file is a design-substrate ledger: observed visual micro-patterns plus generator rules, each annotated with source artifact and compression depth.
questions: How are conflicts between source-specific rules, such as overlay/no-overlay covers, resolved?
What is the intended consumer of these findings: a design generator, a reviewer, or a style-guide compiler?
Are emergent findings meant to be promoted into generator rules or kept as observations?
How do _src values map to actual files or assets?
Is there a process for retiring rules when source decks are updated?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is meant to document subtle visual patterns and their generator rules, and it does so entry by entry. It also reads as a raw extraction because it contains source-specific contradictions and WIP placeholders, so it is partly an emerging style guide rather than a fully reconciled system.
apparent_themes: Visual properties are semantic, not decorative.
Substrate textures create depth without distraction.
Chrome modes signal narrative or register shifts.
Compression depth changes how the same language is expressed.
Document chrome stays light while product UI chrome is dark.
Photo treatment functions as a rhetorical device.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to codify the brand's implicit visual grammar into reproducible generator instructions for pitch and product materials.
novelty: It maps low-level visual channels such as opacity, gradient direction, and hatch angle to semantic meaning, and it explicitly flags patterns as emergent versus specified.
contribution: A structured catalog of visual micro-rules and their contextual sources that a fused design system can draw on when rendering backgrounds, overlays, photo modes, device mockups, stat bands, and chrome-mode transitions.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON array of subtle visual-design findings from pitch decks and landing pages, each describing a pattern, observed recipe, evidence, emergent status, generator rule, source artifact, and compression depth.
intention_for_embedding: To capture and codify the brand's implicit visual grammar so it can be reused as prescriptive generator rules across pitch and web materials.
